title: rosa canina f. plusglandulosa
intro: Rosa canina f. plusglandulosa, also known as the Rosa canina var. plusglandulosa, is a variant of the Rosa canina, commonly known as the dog rose. It is a shrub native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, known for its medicinal properties and various health benefits. The plant's hips, leaves, and flowers have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating wounds, reducing fever, and alleviating digestive issues. Rosa canina f. plusglandulosa can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being used topically in salves and creams.
